topic: auto-ingest shard-name collision pattern (recurring) — read-only archeology + 3-fix-path proposal

background.trigger: ω-cycle atlas-absorb-uncited-batch-3-top-100 (just completed, ~1070s wall) hit recurring collision: substantive shard at atlas.append.atlas-absorb-uncited-batch-3-top-100.n6 was deterministically overwritten by witness-derived 16-fact emit (same as batch-2). Substantive shard restored under non-conflicting filename atlas.append.n6arch-domain-compute-batch3-2026-04-27.n6.
background.root_cause_class: naming-collision between (a) cycle-author hand-curated multi-entry shard and (b) auto-ingest witness-derived shard — both deterministically resolve to atlas.append.<slug>.n6 per the slug-from-witness-filename rule.
background.linked_audit: design/kick/2026-04-27_atlas-shard-naming-convention-audit-cross-session_omega_cycle.json (#15) which proposed C3 two-tier (tool-emitted no-date, manual archive dated).

kernel.intake: Recurring collision class: cycle wants substantive output AND auto-ingest writes witness-derived shard at same path → second write wins, substantive lost.
falsifiers_pre_registered: F1: collision instances located with byte-level evidence (line counts, ts deltas)
F2: ingest-tool collision rule identified verbatim from omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa source
F3: per-instance workaround documented (was collision detected? what filename did the rescue shard adopt?)
F4: 3 fix paths spec'd with honest pros/cons including hidden costs
F5: recommendation backed by frequency-of-occurrence and engineering-cost evidence

axes_fanout.summary: enumerate every collision instance + workaround across 138 omega_cycle witnesses + 166 shards
design 3 fix paths (X cycle-author convention, Y tool-side suffix change, Z hybrid append-detect) and evaluate

kernel.tier1_gate: F1+F2+F3 PASS for archeology; F4+F5 PASS for proposal. If frequency low (<5 instances in 138 cycles) recommendation is workaround+doc, not code change.

ingest_tool_collision_rule.rule: fn _shard_path_for(cycle_slug: string) -> string { let h = env_var("HOME"); return h + "/core/nexus/n6/atlas.append." + cycle_slug + ".n6" }
ingest_tool_collision_rule.slug_derivation: fn _slugify_cycle(witness_path) — strip leading YYYY-MM-DD_ + trailing _omega_cycle, replace _ with -; sanitized basename of witness JSON
ingest_tool_collision_rule.write_op: line 811: write_file(shard_path, shard_text) — UNCONDITIONAL OVERWRITE. No file-exists pre-check; no append-detect; no collision guard.
ingest_tool_collision_rule.idempotence_basis: raw 68 byte-eq idempotence: same witness JSON → byte-identical shard_text → safe re-runs OF AUTO-INGEST. But: any non-auto-ingest content at shard_path is destroyed on next run.
ingest_tool_collision_rule.deterministic_predictability: given a witness JSON at design/**/<YYYY-MM-DD>_<slug>_omega_cycle.json, the resulting shard path is 100% deterministic — collision is in principle pre-detectable before cycle authoring.

collision_instance_table.collision_detected: YES — substantive content explicitly notes ‘Parent witness’ pointer back to batch-2 witness
collision_instance_table.workaround_applied: rename to topic-content-derived slug (n6arch-domains-cognitive-compute) + add YYYY-MM-DD suffix
collision_instance_table.evidence: both files present in working tree (untracked); auto-ingest shard has standard tool-emitted header, substantive shard has hand-curated #!/usr/bin/env n6 + multi-paragraph rationale

collision_instance_table.collision_detected: YES — substantive shard header explicitly states ‘this shard's filename is intentionally DISTINCT from the witness slug to avoid auto-ingest clobber’
collision_instance_table.workaround_applied: same rescue pattern as batch-2 (referenced verbatim in shard comment); ts gap shows substantive shard authored 5 min AFTER auto-ingest emit
collision_instance_table.evidence: substantive shard self-documents the workaround inline (lines 11-16 of header)

collision_instance_table.substantive_rescue: 5 dated companion shards: papers-tecsl-deep-batch-2-2026-04-27.n6, tecsl-archive-2026-04-27.n6, tecsl-archive-deep-batch-2-2026-04-27.n6, sedi-archive-2026-04-27.n6, sedi-archive-deep-batch-2-2026-04-27.n6
collision_instance_table.collision_detected: YES — implicit; cycle-author chose 5 distinct content-derived slugs rather than write to witness-derived path
collision_instance_table.workaround_applied: same rescue pattern: substantive content lives at content-derived slugs + YYYY-MM-DD; auto-ingest shard remains as the witness summary
collision_instance_table.evidence: 5 shards present alongside auto-ingest emit; same-day timestamps; no overlap

all_other_cycles_audit.method: enumerated 138 *_omega_cycle.json witnesses across nexus/anima/canon; cross-referenced with 166 atlas.append.*.n6 shards + atlas_shard_naming_convention_audit_2026-04-27.md (verdict 0 slug collisions)
all_other_cycles_audit.older_batch_cycles_M3_M5_R4_R10_cross_en: did NOT collide. Their substantive shards are dated (e.g. atlas.append.m3-deeper-2026-04-26.n6, r4-deeper, cross-engine-r10-deeper) and were authored BEFORE the witness JSON triggered any auto-ingest run for that exact slug — the witness-derived slugs are different (e.g. m3_anchor_workflow → atlas.append.m3-anchor-workflow.n6). No name overlap by accident.
all_other_cycles_audit.frequency: 3 confirmed instances out of 138 omega_cycle witnesses → 2.2% rate. All 3 are 2026-04-27 cycles producing both witness summary AND deep substantive content (the ‘batch-N’ + ‘archive-deep’ class).
all_other_cycles_audit.structural_predictor: collision happens iff (a) cycle author wants both witness summary auto-ingest AND substantive multi-entry shard, AND (b) substantive shard was named after the cycle topic (matching witness slug). Pure tool-only cycles (no substantive output) and pure manual archive cycles (no witness JSON) never collide.

fix_paths.name: Cycle-author convention
fix_paths.description: Cycles that emit substantive shards MUST use a distinct content-derived slug (with YYYY-MM-DD suffix per C3) and never name the shard after the cycle topic. Auto-ingest shard remains as the witness-summary canonical artifact.
fix_paths.code_changes_required: ZERO in tool/. Documentation-only addition (state/auto_ingest_collision_pattern_2026-04-27.md + atlas.n6 L0 header note).
pros: matches existing de-facto practice (3/3 instances already used this pattern as rescue)
no risk to raw 68 byte-eq idempotence
no risk to raw 77 append-only ledger
trivial to explain in C3 (already aligned: tool no-date, manual dated)
cons: relies on cycle-author discipline; new contributors may forget
no automated guard — collision still possible (silent overwrite) if author names substantive shard after witness slug
honesty: this IS the current pattern; formalizing it adds nothing operationally without enforcement

fix_paths.name: Auto-ingest tool change — dedicated emit suffix
fix_paths.description: Modify _shard_path_for() to emit at atlas.append.<slug>.witness.n6 (or .auto.n6) so the auto-ingest namespace is disjoint from manual shard namespace.
fix_paths.code_changes_required: tool/omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa _shard_path_for one-line change + selftest update + retro-rename of 130 existing tool-emitted shards (or grandfather old + new pattern for new emits — ambiguity for atlas_health_timeline.jsonl, design/spectra/g_atlas_composite_v3.json, README references already cataloging current paths).
pros: structural separation — collision impossible by construction
namespace makes shard origin self-documenting (.witness suffix)
C3 enforcement becomes mechanical (tool-emitted == ends in .witness.n6)
cons: BREAKS raw 68 byte-eq for prior runs (existing 130 tool-emitted shards have different paths than tool would now emit)
BREAKS atlas.n6 absorption history references — past absorbed-from comments in atlas.n6 cite current paths
BREAKS atlas_shard_naming_convention_audit inventory + atlas_health_timeline + g_atlas_composite_v3.json shard-list refs
cascading rename audit: 130+ shards × multiple downstream consumers

fix_paths.name: Hybrid — auto-ingest detects existing shard, appends instead of overwrites
fix_paths.description: Modify auto-ingest: if shard_path already exists AND content does not match expected auto-ingest header signature, treat existing as ‘manual content’, emit auto-ingest body to a sibling .auto.n6 path; if header matches expected signature (re-run case), overwrite is idempotent (raw 68 preserved).
fix_paths.code_changes_required: tool/omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa: add file_exists check + header-signature heuristic before write_file at line 811; emit alternative path on detection. Adds ~20 lines + selftest.
pros: automated collision detection without external discipline
preserves existing 130 shard paths (no rename burden)
fail-safe: substantive content never silently overwritten
cons: HEADER-SIGNATURE HEURISTIC IS FRAGILE — header text changes break detection (false-overwrite of legitimate auto-ingest re-emit OR false-divert of new auto-ingest emit). Must be versioned and stable.

raw 68 byte-eq idempotence becomes path-dependent: same witness re-run could go to different path on different runs depending on filesystem state. This is a SEMANTIC regression — idempotence was previously unconditional.
introduces TWO shard files for one cycle (auto + manual) — inventory audit count complicates
raw 77 append-only respected (we don't mutate existing) but the divert behavior is nondeterministic from cycle-author POV
selftest must cover collision-detected path, which doubles test surface

rationale: frequency is 3/138 = 2.2% — fix should be proportional to incidence

all 3 instances were resolved correctly by cycle-author within minutes (5 min gap on batch-3) — no data loss in any instance (substantive shards survive in working tree, just under different filenames)
X aligns with C3 (recommended in #15 audit): tool-emitted no-date, manual archive YYYY-MM-DD — by definition X-style separates the namespaces
Y carries cascading rename burden across 130 shards + multiple downstream JSONLs / composites — disproportionate cost
Z introduces nondeterminism in idempotence semantics + fragile header heuristic — net loss for the 2.2% case
honest assessment: the workaround pattern IS the canonical solution; formalizing it as written policy + adding a one-line lint check (optional) covers the gap
implementation_notes: doc cycle (not impl cycle): state/auto_ingest_collision_pattern_2026-04-27.md captures the pattern + names it

optional later: hexa lint tool that detects pre-collision (cycle author about to write atlas.append.<slug>.n6 where <slug> matches a pending witness JSON in design/) — small audit-style tool, not a runtime guard
recommendation.honesty_caveat: If the rate spikes above ~5% (e.g., next 5 cycles all collide), revisit and consider Z with a more robust collision-detection mechanism (e.g., manifest-based rather than header heuristic). Currently the data does not justify code change.

outcome.verdict: PASS — 3 collision instances located, ingest-tool rule documented verbatim, 3 fix paths spec'd with honest pros/cons, recommendation X (workaround formalization) backed by 2.2% frequency

carry_forward.doc_cycle_recommended: state/auto_ingest_collision_pattern_2026-04-27.md (this kick will produce it) — formalizes X as canonical workaround
carry_forward.no_impl_cycle_recommended: tool/omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa stays as-is; rationale documented under recommendation.honesty_caveat
carry_forward.linked_audit_followup: C3 written-policy formalization (audit cycle #15 deferred carry-forward item 1) — this kick provides the empirical evidence supporting C3 by demonstrating the collision class C3 prevents

hard_policy_compliance.honesty_path_z_breaks_idempotence: DOCUMENTED — fix_paths[2].cons explicitly states raw 68 byte-eq regression
